---
title: |
  Mathematical Foundations of AI & ML<br>Unit 8: Loss Functions and Regularization
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - Mathematical Foundations of AI & ML"
---

## 01. Title: Loss Functions and Regularization
- Unit role and scope.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Choose losses and regularizers deliberately.

## 03. Recap from Unit 7
- Bayesian/MAP connection.

## 04. Empirical risk minimization
- Objective formulation.

## 05. Loss as decision proxy
- Utility and penalties.

## 06. Regression losses overview
- MSE, MAE, Huber.

## 07. MSE geometry
- Smooth optimization behavior.

## 08. MAE robustness
- Outlier tolerance.

## 09. Huber compromise
- Robustness vs smoothness.

## 10. Classification losses
- 0-1, logistic, hinge.

## 11. Cross-entropy intuition
- Probabilistic consistency.

## 12. Margin-based view
- Separation and confidence.

## 13. Proper scoring rules
- Calibration-aware objectives.

## 14. Class imbalance handling
- Weighted/focal losses.

## 15. Multi-label loss setup
- Independent vs coupled outputs.

## 16. Structured losses (light)
- Sequence/spatial contexts.

## 17. Overfitting reminder
- Capacity vs data.

## 18. Regularization motivation
- Control complexity.

## 19. L2 regularization
- Weight shrinkage intuition.

## 20. L1 regularization
- Sparsity induction.

## 21. Elastic net
- L1 + L2 tradeoff.

## 22. MAP interpretation
- Priors behind penalties.

## 23. Early stopping
- Implicit regularization.

## 24. Data augmentation as regularizer
- Effective sample growth.

## 25. Dropout intuition
- Ensemble-like effect.

## 26. Batch norm side effects
- Optimization and regularization.

## 27. Label smoothing
- Confidence calibration impact.

## 28. Noise injection methods
- Robustness to perturbations.

## 29. Hyperparameter tuning
- Lambda selection strategy.

## 30. Validation-driven model selection
- Honest comparison setup.

## 31. Bias-variance framing
- Regularization sweet spot.

## 32. Underfit vs overfit diagnostics
- Learning curves.

## 33. Double descent hint
- Modern behavior preview.

## 34. Robust optimization preview
- Adversarial robustness link.

## 35. Loss landscape intuition
- Curvature and conditioning.

## 36. Optimization interaction
- GD with different losses.

## 37. Calibration and loss choice
- NLL vs margin losses.

## 38. Domain-specific loss design
- Physics penalties preview.

## 39. Materials example: rare defect cost
- Asymmetric penalties.

## 40. Materials example: smooth property regression
- Huber/L2 comparison.

## 41. Materials example: sparse feature model
- L1 use case.

## 42. Common pitfalls
- Misaligned objective and metric.

## 43. Checklist for loss selection
- Data + decision + constraints.

## 44. Exercise 1 scaffold
- Compare regression losses.

## 45. Exercise 2 scaffold
- L1/L2 regularization sweep.

## 46. Exercise 3 scaffold
- Imbalanced classification setup.

## 47. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield concepts.

## 48. Summary table
- Losses vs regularizers map.

## 49. Unit summary
- Core takeaways.

## 50. References + transition
- Bridge to convex optimization.

::: {#refs}
:::
